_comment: Quality-guardrail ratchet baseline. Metrics may only DECREASE without review; an INCREASE fails CI (see ci/check_quality_guardrails.py). #721 (startup.rs extraction) then merged, removing ~227 lines from the bin — bin line ceiling tightened 4186→3959 to lock that gain, and the new SG-008 startup.rs is now tracked (line ceiling + panic_budget 0, keeping the fail-closed predicate panic-free) and added to ownership_scope. WS-0.5 (/metrics, PR #774, reviewed+merged) grew three tracked files with observability code and its tests: the bin gained the LockedOut scrape DoD test (3997→4087 lines; panic budget 117→121 — the four new expect/unwraps are all in that test), verifier.rs gained the AppState fleet_metrics field (1092→1099), policy_layer.rs gained the denial classifier + counting + two test modules (1036→1154). Fix-PR D (#772 F3, audit-durability observability) added the `incident_durability_failures` counter: verifier.rs gained the AppState field + doc (1099→1108), and the bin's LockedOut-scrape DoD test gained one expected-series line (4087→4088). No new panics in either file (the counter field is not a panic site). Fix-PR E (#774 F1, silent scrape resolver) added `resolve_posture_snapshot_silent` to the bin's posture_engine_v2 import, expanding the single-line `use` to three lines (4088→4090); no new panics. (policy_layer.rs shrank — `classify_gate_denial` was deleted in favour of the authoritative `route_command_verdict` — so its ceiling stays.) Ceilings re-pinned at exactly those values — the next growth still fails. When a refactor legitimately raises a ceiling, bump it with a justification in the PR; when it lowers one, tighten it here. G7 key/identity lifecycle (per-principal tokens #802, RBAC #803, attribution #804) grew the bin with the auth + record-admin-action attribution middleware and their tests (4090→4241, no new panics). G7 transport-security enforcement (#805, the mesh-mTLS option) grew the bin with the `require_secure_transport` middleware + the router-level ordering tests (4241→4382; panic 121→126 — the five new expect/unwraps are all in the router test module) and grew verifier.rs with `TransportSecurityConfig` + the pure `request_transport_is_secure` predicate + its unit tests (1108→1233; panic 27→28 — the one new unwrap is a `HeaderValue::from_str` in a test). Auth-middleware extraction (#721 pattern) then moved the auth path — admin-token auth + RBAC, transport identity/security, admin-action attribution, and their G7 tests — out of the bin into the new tracked `auth.rs` submodule: the bin TIGHTENS 4382→4079 lines and 126→121 panics (the five test expect/unwraps moved with the router tests) to lock the gain; `auth.rs` is newly tracked at 328 lines / panic_budget 5 (all five are in the moved router tests — the production auth code is panic-free) and added to ownership_scope. WS-1 UNIFICATION (cherry-pick of the advisor authz branch + retirement of the env registry): the bin grew 4079→4139 (principals mint/list/revoke routes, the carved-out auditor_routes group, scope-layer comments) and auth.rs grew 328→445 with the unified authorize_scope engine + four scope wrappers + AuthenticatedPrincipal (panic 5→7 — both new expect/unwraps are in the added auditor-gate router test). Counterweight: security.rs shrank ~570→181 (the retired env registry); security.rs is not ratchet-tracked, so the net repo-wide LOC change is strongly negative. WS-1 Track 1.2 (opt-in in-process TLS termination, docs/safety/TRANSPORT_SECURITY.md §4) grew the bin 4139→4176 with the pre-bind fail-closed TLS-config resolution + the serve-mode branch (no new panics — both error arms exit(1)) and added the new tracked auth-sibling tls.rs (321 lines / panic_budget 17 — all 17 expect/unwraps are in the #[cfg(test)] module: the resolve_tls truth table, the config-loader cases, and the live-handshake test; the production resolve/load/serve code is panic-free). Copilot #810 hardening: serve_tls bounds each handshake with HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT and drains in-flight connection tasks via a JoinSet up to DRAIN_GRACE on shutdown (285→321). tls.rs added to ownership_scope. WS-1 Track 1.2 mTLS client-cert → principal: tls.rs grew 321→521 (client-CA WebPkiClientVerifier config + peer_cert_fingerprint extraction/injection + the two live mTLS tests; panic 17→36, all 19 new expect/unwraps in the #[cfg(test)] live-mTLS tests — production code stays panic-free), auth.rs grew 445→478 (the no-bearer cert-fingerprint resolution fallback in authorize_scope; panic unchanged at 7), and the bin grew 4176→4181 (the two admin cert-principal routes). cert_principals store module + the authz cert-path predicate branch are not ratchet-tracked files. Copilot #811 hardening: tls.rs 521→523 (path-generic load_cert_chain error, shared server/CA helper) and auth.rs 478→488 (resolved_via_cert flag → mtls-cert vs api-token credential in the Allow log); no new panics. STOP-GATE REVIEW H3 (HA split-brain fence): policy_layer.rs 1154→1236 — the WriteState/SystemMutation arm of enforce_posture_routing now gates on `mode_active` FIRST via the new pure `mutation_fence_verdict` predicate + `MutationFence` enum (a demoted/standby instance is refused independent of the epoch cache, which is defeated on a disk-wedge self-demotion where cached_db_epoch freezes == held_epoch), plus two exhaustive unit tests over (is_active × held × db). STOP-GATE REVIEW H1 (over-acceleration when steering also clamps): policy_layer.rs 1245→1286 — the frozen kinematics contract gained an `EnforceAction::ClampBoth { linear, steering }` verdict (the `(v_clamped, delta_clamped) == (true, true)` cell previously returned ClampSteering and DROPPED the velocity correction, forwarding the original over-accelerating speed). The deployed consumer gained the matching `EnforcementOutcomeKind::ClampBoth` + `as_str` + `EnforcementOutcome::clamp_both` constructor + the ClampBoth dispatch arm (overwrites BOTH fields). Panic budget unchanged at 14 (the new arm uses `?` + tracing, no new unwrap/expect/panic). The M1 reverse-sign fix + the ClampBoth ripple across the other EnforceAction consumers (kirra-core capture/contract_consumer/kinematics_sim, kirra-trajectory, governor-service, wire-client mirror, parko-kirra diverse) are in non-ratchet-tracked files. STOP-GATE REVIEW H2 (promoted-standby posture freshness): the bin grew 4181→4220 — the Active-mode posture-freshness wiring (posture-engine worker + telemetry watchdog + periodic refresh loop + local-asset feed) was extracted from the inline `if is_active()` startup block into a new shared `wire_active_posture_freshness(&Arc<ServiceState>)` helper (heavily doc-commented) so the standby→Active promotion path can (re)start those tasks too via an on-promote hook threaded into `spawn_promotion_monitor`; without it a runtime-promoted node's cache went stale one TTL after promotion and every gated route fail-closed. Net +39 lines is the helper's doc block minus the now-collapsed startup block; panic budget unchanged at 121 (the helper uses tracing + `std::process::exit` on the fail-closed double-set, no new unwrap/expect/panic). Panic budget unchanged at 14 (the predicate is panic-free; the tests use assert_eq). Copilot #816 follow-up: the two hand-picked mutation_fence tests were consolidated into one loop-driven test that drives the predicate over the FULL finite domain {active,standby}×{0,1,2}² (18 cases), each case's expected verdict computed independently from the H3 spec — making the doc/test "exhaustive" claim literally true rather than softening the wording (1236→1245, +9 lines; panic budget unchanged at 14 — the loop uses assert_eq, no new panic site). WS-4 / TRACK 3 (Fleet Plane — OTA governor-artifact campaign control plane): the bin grew 4220→4430 with the campaign route mounts (create/list/get/arm/advance/halt on admin_routes) + the four real-router campaign tests (fail-closed-without-credential, the end-to-end lifecycle + fail-closed posture-halt, the cold-posture halt, and the validation/error-mapping test) and their `campaign_router`/`campaign_req` helpers; panic budget 121→125 — the four new expect/unwraps are all in the `campaign_req` helper + the halt test's posture-cache write, i.e. #[cfg(test)] only (the campaign handlers are panic-free). The pure engine (`src/ota_campaign.rs`), its store module (`src/verifier_store/ota_campaigns.rs`) and the handler submodule (`src/bin/kirra_verifier_service/campaigns.rs`, State<Arc<ServiceState>> throughout) are not line/panic ratchet-tracked; campaigns.rs is added to ownership_scope. WS-4 / TRACK 3 follow-up (OTA campaign posture-sweep monitor): the bin grew 4430→4445 — `wire_active_posture_freshness` now also spawns the `campaign_monitor` background sweep (so a promoted standby runs it too), auto-halting active campaigns on a CONFIRMED fleet regression between explicit advances; no new panics (the spawn block is tracing + Arc clones). The monitor engine (`src/campaign_monitor.rs`) + the `load_active_campaigns` store query are in non-ratchet-tracked files. WS-4 / TRACK 3 follow-up (node artifact-assignment query): the bin grew 4445→4544 — the public read-only `/fleet/campaigns/assignment/{node_id}` mount plus the four real-router tests (Nominal resolve + cohort miss, LockedOut posture-gating denial) and their `seed_rolling_campaign`/`assignment_req` helpers; panic budget 125→131 — the six new expect/unwraps are all in those #[cfg(test)] helpers (the handler is panic-free). The engine resolver (`resolve_node_assignment`/`is_node_rolled` in `src/ota_campaign.rs`) and the assignment handler (in the already-tracked `fleet.rs`, State<Arc<ServiceState>>) carry the logic; the handler is a GET (classifies ReadTelemetry, posture-gated, no classifier change).
